name: Distributed Training
tagline: Trains large models across multiple accelerators and machines
problem:
Single machines have limited memory and compute. Large models and datasets require parallelism to finish training in reasonable time.

Distributed training solves this by splitting computation and data across devices, but introduces new failure modes: synchronization bugs, silent corruption, and infrastructure fragility.
overview:
Common strategies include data parallelism (replicate model, split data) and model parallelism (split model across devices). Communication bandwidth and latency become bottlenecks.

A practical distributed system needs checkpointing, monitoring, and deterministic configuration management. The Codex treats this as both a software and infrastructure technology.

principles.name: Parallelism strategies
principles.explanation: Data/model/pipeline parallelism trade communication for memory and speed.

principles.name: Synchronization
principles.explanation: Gradient averaging and parameter updates require consistent coordination.

principles.name: Fault tolerance
principles.explanation: Failures are normal at scale; recovery must be designed in.

rawMaterials.name: Electric power
rawMaterials.purpose: Energy for computation, storage, and networking
earthLocations: Electrical grids globally; generators in remote sites
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Solar + batteries is the simplest early-space pathway; plan for tight energy budgets and thermal rejection.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Stable voltage regulation, grounding, and basic power-quality control.

rawMaterials.name: Semiconductor devices
rawMaterials.purpose: Transistors and ICs that perform switching, storage, and signal conditioning
earthLocations: Global semiconductor supply chains; packaged IC distributors
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Early colonies import packaged devices; long-term local manufacture requires clean chemistry and crystal growth capabilities.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: ESD-safe handling; correct packaging, heat sinking, and derating for temperature and radiation.

rawMaterials.name: Documentation and standards
rawMaterials.purpose: Make designs reproducible and debuggable across teams and time
earthLocations: Libraries, universities, standards bodies, archived web
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Mirror core references offline; maintain printed and digital copies with checksums and redundancy.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Versioning, change control, and clear measurement units and assumptions.

rawMaterials.name: Network equipment
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Bandwidth and power constraints push toward smaller models and more efficient training; prioritize robustness over peak throughput.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Cabling discipline, configuration management, and monitoring.

buildSteps.title: Choose parallelism strategy
buildSteps.description: Pick data vs model vs pipeline parallelism based on model size and network.
buildSteps.warningNote: Wrong strategy wastes most compute on communication.

buildSteps.title: Implement fault-tolerant checkpoints
buildSteps.description: Checkpoint frequently; test resume; keep metadata stable.
buildSteps.warningNote: No resume plan means failed runs waste weeks.

buildSteps.title: Instrument training
buildSteps.description: Monitor throughput, errors, and divergence; alert on anomalies.

buildSteps.title: Validate determinism and configs
buildSteps.description: Pin software versions and configs; track seeds and dataset versions.

history.event: Distributed systems mature in industry and HPC.
Large-model training becomes a systems engineering field.

impact:
Distributed training enables large models but increases fragility and governance risks. It concentrates capability in organizations that can maintain complex infrastructure.

In The Codex, it is near the top of the AI chain: do not attempt it without strong monitoring, evaluation, and safety governance.
